claims.evidenceExcerpt: They're incredible at generating things that sound correct. But are they correct? And how do you know that they're correct is a big problem.
claims.text: The speaker argues that the main challenge with AI is not making plausible output, but reliably knowing whether it is correct, safe, and useful.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: But the critical caveat when you read the data is this is at a 50% success rate. Okay. So it is again able to complete tasks but is it able to complete tasks correctly is the question.
claims.text: He reports that coding agents can complete much longer tasks than before, but benchmarked success still depends heavily on accuracy rather than raw task length.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: And what you see with even the state-of-the-art models is that complexity is still high. It's actually quite variable as you can see here.
claims.text: He says state-of-the-art coding models still produce high complexity, bugs, and security issues, so agent output is not enterprise-ready by default.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: And if you ask why, it is because of the two pieces in red here that you start to see there's an increase in velocity, but there's an increase in security issues, there's an increase in maintainability issues, there's an increase in reliability issues, and there's an increase in complexity.
claims.text: He argues that initial productivity gains from AI coding agents can fade as security, maintainability, reliability, and complexity debt accumulate.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: And if you bake it into the process of generating code, of doing software development, you can actually start to get materially better outcomes from the coding agents than if you view it as an afterthought.
claims.text: He claims that baking verification into the development process produces materially better outcomes than treating it as an afterthought.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: So as we look at our partners and customers who use a multi-layered verification approach they are reporting AI derived production outages being 44% less frequent than the ones who do not.
claims.text: He reports that multi-layered verification lowered AI-derived production outages by 44% in partner and customer environments.

claims.evidenceExcerpt: And one of the tests we did with one of the large banks who are using some of the cutting edge the folks who are all around here today um cutting edge agentic coding tools they can get a 92% reduction in issues if you actually take this guide verify solve approach inside of those agentic loops.
claims.text: He reports that a guide-verify-solve approach inside agentic loops produced a 92% reduction in issues for one large bank.

payload.methodNotes: The framework is presented as AC/DC, an agent-centric development cycle that centers verification inside the software lifecycle rather than bolting it on afterward.

methods.description: The speaker's core operating model for agentic software development: guide the agent, verify the output, then solve the problem.
methods.evidenceExcerpt: Guide the agents, verify the outcomes and then solve the problems. And you have to make this part of the discipline, part of the process, part of the new software development life cycle if you want to be successful in the AI world.
methods.name: Guide, Verify, Solve

methods.description: Using repository context plus explicit constraints to steer agents before they generate code.
methods.evidenceExcerpt: What we find is critically important is to think about guide as context and constraints and we separate out context and constraints very deliberately because context is you have your code repositories.
methods.name: Context and Constraints

methods.description: Combining algorithmic checks with agentic checks and assuming no single verifier is sufficient.
methods.evidenceExcerpt: How do you make sure there's less to verify, less to fix, this sort of thing. Then you get to the heart of verification and what we believe quite strongly and what we've seen work in practice is this idea of zero trust multi-layered verification.
methods.name: Zero-Trust Multi-Layered Verification

methods.description: Maintaining code actively and verifying maintenance work so technical debt does not compound unchecked.
methods.evidenceExcerpt: And again, this is not stop doing it. This is be aware and let's start controlling it. And so what we um have seen be super effective is to have an active process to have an active discipline again around code maintenance and thinking about how you do verified code maintenance.
methods.name: Verified Code Maintenance

methods.description: Running agentic work, CI verification, and maintenance as linked loops that reinforce each other.
methods.evidenceExcerpt: So you have your your code maintenance loop, agentic loop, CI verification loop and deliberate design of these loops with verification at the center is a compounding system.
methods.name: Three-Loop Development Cycle

questions.evidenceExcerpt: Software has lots of of of intricacies involved with it. And so what we believe and again have found to be quite um impactful here is that a combination of algorithmic verification looking at things like data flows, control flows, known patterns, secrets, these areas combined with what is now possible with agentic verification looking at intent, business logic, the unknown unknowns.
questions.question: What mix of algorithmic verification and agentic verification best covers different classes of defects in a given codebase?
questions.whyItMatters: The talk implies that different failure modes need different checks, but it does not specify how to allocate them in practice.

questions.evidenceExcerpt: Um, we think this is something that compounds. But if you just do the exact same agentic tasks on a typical codebase and then one that has been cleaned, you see a material reduction in the amount of tokens, reasoning, energy, etc.
questions.question: How can teams keep large codebases clean enough that agents remain efficient as technical debt accumulates?
questions.whyItMatters: The speaker argues that cleaner code makes agents cheaper and more reliable, but the maintenance strategy is left open.

questions.evidenceExcerpt: There's a second which is your continuous improvement process and how do you really combine the power of algorithmic and agentic to generate your your pull request review the code and by the way the velocity of this has to go up massively so to review the code using agents and to do this multi-layered
questions.question: How should inner-loop verification, CI review, and evaluation gates be balanced so agent velocity does not outrun quality?
questions.whyItMatters: The talk treats these loops as a system, but the operational design choices are not fully resolved.

payload.summary: The talk argues that the core bottleneck in agentic software development is no longer code generation but verification: models can now produce long, plausible outputs, but correctness, security, and maintainability still lag. The proposed answer is an agent-centric development cycle that wraps generation in guide, verify, solve loops, using context and constraints, zero-trust multi-layered checks, and ongoing code maintenance to make agents easier to steer and review. The tradeoff is that stronger verification adds process, but the speaker says it pays back in lower token usage, fewer outages, and lower technical debt, especially in large existing codebases where machine-speed debt can erase early productivity gains. In practice, the talk presents verification infrastructure as both a safety requirement and a competitive advantage.

takeaways.evidenceExcerpt: And if you bake it into the process of generating code, of doing software development, you can actually start to get materially better outcomes from the coding agents than if you view it as an afterthought.
takeaways.text: Build verification into the development process instead of treating it as a post hoc review step.

takeaways.evidenceExcerpt: But what about where you want to go? And so this idea of context and constraints uh we've found in our testing generates a massive improvement in agent effectiveness and a massive uh improvement in token consumption o over 30% reduction in tokens being used to solve a given problem.
takeaways.text: Provide agents with repository context and explicit constraints so they navigate better and waste fewer tokens.

takeaways.evidenceExcerpt: Software has lots of of of intricacies involved with it. And so what we believe and again have found to be quite um impactful here is that a combination of algorithmic verification looking at things like data flows, control flows, known patterns, secrets, these areas combined with what is now possible with agentic verification looking at intent, business logic, the unknown unknowns.
takeaways.text: Use multiple verification layers because no single model or technique is enough for complex software.

takeaways.evidenceExcerpt: And again, this is not stop doing it. This is be aware and let's start controlling it. And so what we um have seen be super effective is to have an active process to have an active discipline again around code maintenance and thinking about how you do verified code maintenance.
takeaways.text: Treat code maintenance as an active discipline, because generated code creates technical debt quickly.

takeaways.evidenceExcerpt: So you have your your code maintenance loop, agentic loop, CI verification loop and deliberate design of these loops with verification at the center is a compounding system.
takeaways.text: Design agentic work, CI verification, and maintenance as reinforcing loops so quality compounds instead of decays.

topics.name: Verification-First Development

topics.name: Multi-Layered Verification

talkTitle: 'In the Land of AI Agents, the Verifiers Are King'
